en_title: Spring Rejoices Beside The Pillow Of Fragrant Moonlight
en_body:
First little passage:

 Moonlit Night lifts her hair, bending to greet,
 Making Poetry shyly hide within fragrance...

(*) Night...inside the room, within four walls are miraculous, subtle openings: one doorway and window frames--the walls already ash-gray while the borders are tender banana-leaf green; the room door painted deep violet, the powdery paint smooth as velvet; the window curtains black with tiny pale-pink flowers and little green leaves...

(*) Night...the moon above hangs hovering, with the hovering of Huệ Thu's verse, "...water flows on, the dream still beside the cliff hangs wavering..." The moon still shines as it has shone from great antiquity, as a thousand miles into the future it shall still shine. Moonlight crystal-clear and tender passes through the realm of Space and the expansion of Time, flowing its long hem into the room. The breath of air moves naturally and indifferently, with a silent countenance, bringing the light in here. Moon and moonlight encounter the regions of color within without the least bewilderment. And thus, in silence, the natural Way of the universe establishes Art--the miraculous and subtle openings of humankind--the exile-realm complex with delicate mysteries. Those exiles who came before made them, sent tidings to their exile-friends coming after--That we may still create here a place of homeland by living truly and living fully; life-force issuing from within releases its luminous essence, creating a transparent living world...Night, a velvet cradle like the landscape of life, nourishes one heart's longing to create. Therefore...Night...and thus, content and form enrich each other, the alluvium of creation enriching the fertile soil of History so Humanity may flower beyond itself. Content brims over, whispering its reminder, restless in urging: O Form! Thank you for demanding the devoted cultivation of the tablet of talent, to help ourselves and those of tomorrow: transcend all boundaries; and carry the language of Love:

(*) Night...like the tightly interlacing fingers of two miraculous, consummate hands: form and content marry and through their faithful cleaving, give birth to and nurture the infinite ache of Love!
note: Huệ Thu is the poet whose quoted line gives the hovering moon its image of a dream suspended beside a cliff.
